title: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette

seo.title: Gyeran Mari (Korean Rolled Omelette) - Easy Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Gyeran Mari, the delicious Korean rolled omelette. This easy step-by-step recipe is perfect for breakfast or a side dish!
longTailKeywords: best Korean rolled omelette at home
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Gyeran Mari
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Gyeran Mari
Pro Tips for the Best Gyeran Mari
Serving Suggestions for Korean Rolled Omelette
seo.imageAlt: A perfectly cooked Gyeran Mari, sliced and stacked, showing the beautiful layers of the Korean rolled omelette.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this fluffy and flavorful Gyeran Mari, a classic Korean rolled omelette perfect for any meal!

description: Gyeran Mari is a beloved Korean rolled omelette, known for its soft texture and beautiful layered appearance. This versatile dish is a staple in Korean households, perfect for breakfast, lunchboxes, or as a delightful banchan (side dish). Its simple yet elegant preparation makes it a comforting and satisfying addition to any meal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Egg Mixture
instructions.text: Crack the eggs into a large bowl. Add the finely chopped carrot, green onion, salt, and black pepper. If desired, add a tablespoon of milk or water for a fluffier texture. Whisk everything together gently until just combined, being careful not to overmix to avoid too many bubbles.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a non-stick rectangular or round pan over medium-low heat. Lightly coat the bottom with about 1 teaspoon of cooking oil, spreading it evenly with a paper towel. Keep the heat low and consistent to prevent the omelette from browning too quickly.

instructions.title: Pour First Layer
instructions.text: Pour approximately one-quarter to one-third of the egg mixture into the heated pan, tilting the pan to create a thin, even layer. Let it cook until the bottom is set but the top is still slightly wet. This usually takes about 2-3 minutes.

instructions.title: Start Rolling
instructions.text: Once the egg layer is mostly set but still pliable, use a spatula to gently roll the omelette from one end to the other, forming a tight log. Push the rolled omelette to one side of the pan, leaving space for the next layer.

instructions.title: Add Subsequent Layers
instructions.text: Re-oil the empty part of the pan lightly if needed. Pour another portion of the egg mixture into the pan, making sure some of it seeps underneath the rolled omelette. Let this new layer cook until mostly set, then roll the existing omelette over this new layer, continuing to build the roll. Repeat this process until all the egg mixture is used, ensuring each layer cooks properly before rolling.

instructions.title: Cook Through and Shape
instructions.text: Once all the egg is rolled, gently press the entire omelette roll with the spatula on all sides to ensure it is cooked through and holds its shape. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and firm. This ensures a uniform texture.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the Gyeran Mari to a cutting board. Let it rest for a minute or two, then slice it into 1-inch thick pieces using a sharp knife. Arrange the slices neatly on a plate and serve warm with a side of soy sauce or ketchup, if desired. It makes a wonderful presentation.

tips: Use a rectangular pan if possible for a perfectly uniform roll, but a round non-stick pan works just as well with careful shaping.
For extra flavor and color, you can add finely diced bell peppers, ham, or seaweed flakes to the egg mixture. Ensure all additions are very finely chopped.
Keep the heat low and consistent throughout the cooking process. High heat will brown the egg too quickly and make it difficult to roll without breaking.
article: Gyeran Mari Korean Rolled Omelette is a beloved Korean Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
